name: Tossed Noodles (Szechuan)
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Fresh noodles (or 2 cups Cooked carrots, shredded)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine marinade ingredients: sesame oil, soy sauce, vegetable oil, garlic, red bean paste, sugar, and dry sherry. Mix until smooth.
2. Add the pork cubes to the marinade and let stand for 20 minutes, turning occasionally.
3. Heat oil in a wok over medium heat for 1 minute. Stir-fry marinated pork for 2 minutes until lightly browned. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
4. In the same wok, add remaining oil. Stir-fry onions until tender. Add the seasoning sauce and bring to a boil. Return pork and garlic to the wok; stir-fry until well mixed. Set aside.
5. Bring 3 quarts of water to a rapid boil in a heavy pot. Blanch bean sprouts for 5 seconds, then plunge into ice water. Drain well.
6. Add salt and oil to boiling water. Cook noodles (or carrots) until tender. Drain and divide among 6 bowls.
7. Spoon pork and sauce over the noodles. Garnish with carrots, cucumber, and blanched bean sprouts. Toss lightly before serving.
Yield: 6 bowls
